title: plantago maritima subsp. alpina
intro: Plantago maritima subsp. alpina is a subspecies of the coastal plantain, a perennial herb native to alpine and subalpine regions, characterized by its small, oval leaves and spikes of greenish-brown flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and protecting against oxidative stress, which can contribute to chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The herbal preparations of Plantago maritima subsp. alpina include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
